L1.area: Web session state persistence and data serialization infrastructure

L2.description: An incomplete cryptography vulnerability exists where session data stored in client-side form fields is only Base64-encoded without encryption. This allows remote attackers to obtain the stored session data, register invalid values, or alter session data transmitted between the client and server.

L3.area: Hidden HTML form field-based session storage implementation and its data serialization logic
L3.description: An incomplete cryptography vulnerability exists where session data persisted via hidden HTML input fields is only Base64-encoded without any encryption. This allows remote attackers to decode and read session contents, inject forged session entries, or tamper with session data, as the stored values lack both confidentiality protection and integrity verification.

ground_truth.reason: The HiddenStore session store serialized session entries to hidden HTML form fields using only Base64 encoding without encryption, allowing remote attackers to read, modify, or forge session data.
